ground_truth.title: Software Development Degree Track - Bachelor of Science (BS) in Computer Science with Software Development Track
ground_truth.main_content:
Software Development Degree Track
Bachelor of Science (BS) in Computer Science with Software Development Track
Power The Future Through Code
The Software Development Track prepares students for one of the most in-demand fields in today’s tech-driven world. This track emphasizes hands-on experience in software engineering, database design, web and multimedia technologies, and mobile app development—equipping graduates with the skills to create innovative, real-world solutions across platforms.
Degree Requirements
120 total credit hours required for graduation
48 upper-level credit hours and a minor are required
All required and elective courses must be completed with a grade of C- or better
Computer Science Core (25 hours), Software Development Track (19 hours), and Mathematics (14 hours)
Capstone Course, Freshman Seminar, and 16 hours of General Electives
Careers for Computer Science Majors with a Software Development Track:
The skills you develop here will launch you into nearly any field of including:
Software Developer
Computer Systems Analysts
Computer Support Specialists
Computer Network Architects
Computer Hardware Engineers
Database Administrators
Information Security Analysts
Degree Roadmap
Get all the details on our Computer Science program, including admission requirements, financial considerations, class requirements, and more.
FAQs
What is the Software Development track?
This specialization trains students to build robust software solutions across platforms—covering database design, multimedia/web, mobile apps, software engineering, and large-scale project development.
How many credits are required to graduate?
Students must complete 120 total semester credit hours, including 25 credits of core CS, 19 in the Software Development track, 17 in mathematics, and general education/electives. At least 48 upper-level credits and 30 final credits must be earned at UTPB.
Are there specific grade requirements?
Yes—students must earn a minimum grade of C in all major courses and maintain an overall GPA of at least 2.0 to meet graduation requirements.
What are the key courses in this track?
Major upper-level track courses include:
COSC 4415: Database Systems
COSC 4455: Multimedia & Web Development
COSC 4460: Software Engineering
COSC 4485: Mobile Application Development
COSC 4395: Research/Capstone project
How much math is required?
Students complete 17 credit hours in math, including Calculus I & II, Intro to Probability, and Mathematical Reasoning—or substitute equivalent courses.
Is hands-on experience included in the curriculum?
Absolutely. Students engage in practical, lab-based projects and complete the COSC 4395 capstone, emphasizing real-world software development and portfolio-building.
What career opportunities does this track support?
Graduates are prepared for careers as Software Developers, Database Administrators, Systems Analysts, Front‑End/Web Developers, Mobile App Engineers, and more.

Explore everything Computer Science has to offer at UTPB!
